sentence_reviews.text: A tractable lower bound balances data fit against divergence between approximate and prior distributions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes abstract lines 37-40 describes a variational lower bound estimator, approximate inference model, continuous latent variables, and optimization by stochastic gradient methods.
sentence_reviews.notes: This matches the ELBO structure used in variational autoencoders: reconstruction/data-fit term plus divergence regularization.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of ELBO & variational objectives is to connect Generative modeling, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Variational autoencoders.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes title line 31 and abstract lines 37-40.
sentence_reviews.notes: The paper directly addresses directed probabilistic models with latent variables and introduces the VAE variational lower-bound estimator.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes title line 31 and abstract lines 37-40.
sentence_reviews.notes: The ELBO is the central VAE training objective.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes abstract line 37 describes directed probabilistic models and latent variables; lines 38-40 describe variational learning/inference.
sentence_reviews.notes: VAEs combine variational inference objectives with generative probabilistic models.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Generative modeling, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes abstract lines 37-40 supports tractable lower-bound optimization for latent-variable models.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports lower-bound optimization and dependencies, but not the broader surrogate-mismatch warning exactly.

sentence_reviews.text: ELBO & variational objectives uses Generative modeling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes abstract line 37 references directed probabilistic models; lines 38-40 describe variational learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The ELBO is defined for probabilistic generative models with latent variables.

sentence_reviews.text: ELBO & variational objectives uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes abstract lines 37-39 explicitly mentions continuous latent variables and approximate posterior inference.
sentence_reviews.notes: Latent variables are central to the variational lower-bound setup described by the source.

sentence_reviews.text: ELBO & variational objectives uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE abstract lines 37-40; PML landing page lines 21-29 identifies a probabilistic ML textbook.
sentence_reviews.notes: ELBOs are defined with likelihoods, priors, approximate posteriors, and divergences.

sentence_reviews.text: ELBO & variational objectives uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes abstract lines 38-40 describes optimizing the lower-bound estimator with stochastic gradient methods.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly says the lower-bound estimator can be optimized with stochastic gradient methods.

edge_reviews.notes: ELBO objectives are used to learn probabilistic generative latent-variable models.
Latent variables and approximate posterior inference are directly named in the VAE abstract.
Probability theory is necessary for likelihoods, priors, approximate posteriors, and KL divergence; common strength is defensible though arguably core.
The VAE abstract directly supports optimization of the variational lower-bound estimator using stochastic gradients.
